title: The 25 Best Movies of the Century: No. 18 - ‘Before Sunset’
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins put Before Sunset at No. 18, work through why they take it over Before Sunrise, and use it to run the Linklater century filmography, the Hawke and Delpy careers, and Amanda's list of movies to watch next.

films.title: Before Sunset
films.note: Richard Linklater directs and shares the screenplay with stars Julie Delpy and Ethan Hawke, who both fed their own lives into the dialogue while Hawke was going through a divorce. Nine years after Before Sunrise, Jesse's book tour brings Celine to Shakespeare and Company in Paris, and they wander for the 80-minute runtime before his plane. Near-real-time, long walk-and-talks, international landmarks in the background. Both hosts rank it over Sunrise: a richer text and a bigger performance feat. Sean calls the 'baby, you are gonna miss that plane' close the greatest movie ending of their lifetimes and remembers an Angelika audience screaming 'no' at the cut to black. Amanda counters that Before Midnight has soured her read: the couple's love was already flawed here.

films.title: Before Sunrise
films.note: The origin: two 20-somethings, American Jesse and French Celine, meet on a train to Vienna and spend one night walking the city before Jesse's flight, then part with no contact info and a plan to meet in six months. A '90s American indie built on pure attraction and no real conflict.

films.title: Before Midnight
films.note: The third film, which reveals the couple got together, married, and had a family, and now grinds through a tumultuous marriage. Its sourness is deliberate, and Sean, who hasn't revisited it, admits it took some of the shine off his first experience of Sunset. Twelve years on, there's no sign of a fourth.

segments.heading: The Linklater century filmography
segments.note: the movies they weighed and passed on

films.title: Boyhood
films.note: His most acclaimed film of the century and another experiment in showing time on screen, shot a bit each year for over a decade. Amanda likes it well more than Sean; he finds it as 'real' as Before Sunset but still ranks the trilogy above it.

films.title: School of Rock
films.note: His biggest hit and the movie the generation below them knows him by. Sean was recently told it plays great with small children.

films.title: Everybody Wants Some!!
films.note: Sean's devil's-advocate pick from an earlier version of the list. He'd never take it over Before Sunset, but calls it powerful, and the place he and Amanda first clocked Glen Powell.

films.title: Fast Food Nation
films.note: Filed with Me and Orson Welles as a clear miss with things to recommend it, not the upper echelon.

films.title: Blue Moon
films.note: New Linklater-Hawke collaboration due later in 2025, their first since Boyhood. Linklater is also mid-way through Merrily We Roll Along, a durational project shot over 20 years.

films.title: Two Days in Paris
films.note: Delpy wrote and directed it; Amanda found it very funny. Delpy has directed a handful of films but hasn't been much of a Hollywood linchpin since the trilogy.

films.title: Two Days in New York
films.note: Delpy's follow-up, the one with Chris Rock.

films.title: Training Day
films.note: The most famous example of Hawke's willingness to play number two to a bigger star, and it got him an Oscar nomination. He never chased traditional center-of-the-movie stardom.

films.title: Reality Bites
films.note: Cited as the root of the Hawke persona, the '90s hot-loser slacker he's stayed adjacent to even inside bigger productions.

films.title: The Black Phone
films.note: Hawke as the child-abducting villain who meets a grisly end. He's used horror like this to fund his directorial and TV projects.

films.title: Black Phone 2
films.note: The sequel, out this year, with Hawke's killer reaching back from beyond.

picks.title: Brief Encounter
picks.note: David Lean's film, a big one for Sean and his wife and for Before Sunset heads.

picks.title: The Notebook
picks.note: The gateway pick for the basic romantic who hasn't found the Before films yet.

picks.title: Materialists
picks.note: Recently covered on the show; the hosts flag it as a good fit.

picks.title: Pride & Prejudice
picks.note: The Joe Wright version, being remade again as a Netflix series after the Colin Firth / Jennifer Ehle BBC run.
